etymology.description: Depicts a kind of dragon, either a Panlong (aquatic dragon) or a Chi (dragon without horns) -> used as a decorative motif in ancient Chinese art
etymology.interpretation: Though primarily depicting specific types of dragons, the character 𦝠 was not widely adopted in contemporary language to convey the meaning 'food, eat.' Instead, the depiction reinforced its cultural role associated with mythology and art, with uses reflecting its original artistic significance.
